questions.question: Which Dockerfile instructions create new image layers?
options: RUN, COPY, and ADD
ENV, LABEL, and EXPOSE
CMD, ENTRYPOINT, and WORKDIR
ARG, USER, and VOLUME
questions.answer: RUN, COPY, and ADD

questions.question: What is the main benefit of combining multiple RUN commands into a single RUN instruction?
options: It makes the Dockerfile easier to read
It reduces the number of layers and the overall image size
It ensures commands run in parallel
It prevents caching from being used
questions.answer: It reduces the number of layers and the overall image size

questions.question: Which of the following is the optimized way to install packages in a Dockerfile?
options: Three separate RUN instructions for update, install, and clean
A single RUN instruction chaining update, install, and clean with &&
Using COPY to bring in pre-built binaries
Using ADD to download packages from the internet
questions.answer: A single RUN instruction chaining update, install, and clean with &&

questions.question: What command shows the layers of a Docker image and their sizes?
options: docker inspect
docker layers
docker history
docker info
questions.answer: docker history

questions.question: What is the purpose of a multi-stage build in Docker?
options: To run multiple containers simultaneously
To use one stage for building and another for running, producing a smaller final image
To build images for multiple architectures
To deploy to multiple registries at once
questions.answer: To use one stage for building and another for running, producing a smaller final image

questions.question: In a multi-stage Dockerfile, what does the AS keyword do?
options: Sets an environment variable for the stage
Names a build stage so it can be referenced later
Creates an alias for the base image
Specifies the architecture for the stage
questions.answer: Names a build stage so it can be referenced later

questions.question: How do you copy a file from a previous build stage named 'builder'?
options: COPY --stage=builder /app/myapp /usr/local/bin/
COPY --from=builder /app/myapp /usr/local/bin/
COPY builder:/app/myapp /usr/local/bin/
ADD --from=builder /app/myapp /usr/local/bin/
questions.answer: COPY --from=builder /app/myapp /usr/local/bin/

questions.question: In a multi-stage build, what ends up in the final Docker image?
options: All layers from all stages
Only the layers from the last stage
Only the RUN instructions from all stages
Only the COPY instructions from all stages
questions.answer: Only the layers from the last stage

questions.question: What is the .dockerignore file used for?
options: Listing images that should not be pulled
Specifying containers to ignore during cleanup
Excluding files and directories from the Docker build context
Defining environment variables to ignore at runtime
questions.answer: Excluding files and directories from the Docker build context

questions.question: Which file is .dockerignore most similar to in function?
options: .gitignore
.env
Dockerfile
docker-compose.yml
questions.answer: .gitignore

questions.question: Which of the following should typically be included in a .dockerignore file?
options: app.py and requirements.txt
.git, node_modules, and .env
Dockerfile and CMD instructions
The base image name and tag
questions.answer: .git, node_modules, and .env

questions.question: Why should you copy requirements.txt before copying the rest of your application code?
options: Because requirements.txt must be in the image root directory
To take advantage of Docker's layer caching so dependencies are not reinstalled on every code change
Because COPY can only handle one file at a time
To ensure the application code is not overwritten
questions.answer: To take advantage of Docker's layer caching so dependencies are not reinstalled on every code change

questions.question: What does the --no-cache-dir flag do when used with pip install?
options: Prevents Docker from caching the layer
Forces pip to download packages every time
Prevents pip from storing downloaded packages in its cache, reducing image size
Disables the Docker build cache entirely
questions.answer: Prevents pip from storing downloaded packages in its cache, reducing image size

questions.question: In a multi-stage Java build, why is the final image much smaller than using the full JDK image?
options: Because Java bytecode is compressed automatically
Because the final stage uses only the JRE, excluding the compiler and dev tools
Because multi-stage builds compress all layers into one
Because the AS keyword reduces the image size
questions.answer: Because the final stage uses only the JRE, excluding the compiler and dev tools

questions.question: What does the USER instruction do in a Dockerfile?
options: Creates a new Docker Hub account
Sets the username for logging into the container registry
Specifies which user the container process runs as
Grants sudo access to the container
questions.answer: Specifies which user the container process runs as

questions.question: Why is it a best practice to run containers as a non-root user?
options: It makes the container start faster
It reduces the image size
It improves security by limiting what the process can do if compromised
It is required by Docker Hub for publishing images
questions.answer: It improves security by limiting what the process can do if compromised

questions.question: What does setting PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 accomplish in a Dockerfile?
options: Prevents Python from running in interactive mode
Prevents Python from creating .pyc bytecode files
Disables Python's garbage collector
Forces Python to use Python 2 syntax
questions.answer: Prevents Python from creating .pyc bytecode files

questions.question: What does setting PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 accomplish in a Dockerfile?
options: Disables Python's import system
Prevents Python from using virtual environments
Ensures Python output is sent directly to the terminal without buffering
Forces Python to write all output to a log file
questions.answer: Ensures Python output is sent directly to the terminal without buffering

questions.question: What does the --prefix flag do when used with pip install?
options: Adds a prefix to all installed package names
Installs packages into a specified directory instead of the default location
Specifies a version prefix for all packages
Sets the Python path prefix for import resolution
questions.answer: Installs packages into a specified directory instead of the default location

questions.question: Why is it recommended to use a specific image tag (e.g., python:3.12-slim) instead of 'latest'?
options: The 'latest' tag is slower to download
Specific tags ensure reproducible builds since 'latest' can change unpredictably
The 'latest' tag is deprecated in Docker
Specific tags are smaller in size
questions.answer: Specific tags ensure reproducible builds since 'latest' can change unpredictably

questions.question: What does 'rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' accomplish when added to a RUN instruction?
options: Removes all installed packages
Removes the apt package manager
Removes cached package lists to reduce the layer size
Removes the system's root filesystem
questions.answer: Removes cached package lists to reduce the layer size

questions.question: In the Dockerfile instruction 'COPY --from=builder /install /usr/local', what is being copied?
options: The builder stage's Dockerfile
The /install directory from the builder stage into /usr/local in the current stage
The Docker installation files
The builder stage's environment variables
questions.answer: The /install directory from the builder stage into /usr/local in the current stage

questions.question: What happens if a file listed in .dockerignore is referenced by a COPY instruction in the Dockerfile?
options: Docker automatically creates an empty file in its place
The build fails because the file is not in the build context
Docker ignores the .dockerignore rule and copies the file anyway
Docker logs a warning but continues the build
questions.answer: The build fails because the file is not in the build context

questions.question: Which command creates a non-root user named 'appuser' in a Debian-based Dockerfile?
options: RUN useradd appuser --no-login
RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' appuser
USER appuser --create
RUN create-user appuser
questions.answer: RUN adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' appuser

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using .dockerignore to exclude .git and node_modules from the build context?
options: It prevents Docker from modifying those directories
It speeds up the build and reduces the build context size sent to the Docker daemon
It ensures those directories are backed up before building
It allows Docker to use those directories for caching
questions.answer: It speeds up the build and reduces the build context size sent to the Docker daemon
